theorem valuationShortComplex_shortExact : (valuationShortComplex G K L).ShortExact := by
  have h₁ : Function.Injective (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap := by
    have h : Function.Injective (ker_v L).toModuleHom := by
      apply ModuleHom.injective_iff_ker_eq_bot.mpr
      simp [Submodule.ker_eq_bot]
      <;>
      exact by
        intros x hx
        have : x = 0 := by
          simp_all [Unit.ext_iff]
          <;>
          aesop
        simp [this]
    simpa using h
  
  have h₂ : Function.Surjective (v L).toIntLinearMap := by
    have h : Function.Surjective (v L).toModuleHom := by
      apply ModuleHom.surjective_iff_range_eq_top.mpr
      simp [Submodule.range_eq_top]
      <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try
        {
          intros x
          -- We need to find an element in Lˣ that maps to x under the valuation map.
          -- Since the valuation is surjective onto the integers, for any integer x,
          -- we can find an element in Lˣ whose valuation is x.
          have h₁ : ∃ (y : Lˣ), v L y = x := by
            -- Use the fact that the valuation is surjective.
            have h₂ : Function.Surjective (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) := by
              -- The valuation is surjective.
              exact (IsNonarchimedeanLocalField.valuation L).surjective
            obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := h₂ x
            exact ⟨y, hy⟩
          obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := h₁
          -- Use the element y to show that x is in the range of the valuation map.
          refine' ⟨y, _⟩
          <;> simp_all [ModuleHom.apply_smul]
          <;> aesop
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the fact that the valuation is surjective to find an element in Lˣ that maps to any integer x.
          have h₁ : Function.Surjective (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) := by
            -- The valuation is surjective.
            exact (IsNonarchimedeanLocalField.valuation L).surjective
          intro x
          obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := h₁ x
          refine' ⟨y, _⟩
          <;> simp_all [ModuleHom.apply_smul]
          <;> aesop
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Use the fact that the valuation is surjective to find an element in Lˣ that maps to any integer x.
          have h₁ : Function.Surjective (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) := by
            -- The valuation is surjective.
            exact (IsNonarchimedeanLocalField.valuation L).surjective
          -- Since the valuation is surjective, we can find an element in Lˣ that maps to any integer x.
          -- This shows that the range of the valuation map is the entire set of integers.
          intro x
          obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := h₁ x
          refine' ⟨y, _⟩
          <;> simp_all [ModuleHom.apply_smul]
          <;> aesop
        })
    simpa using h
  
  have h₃ : (ker_v L).toIntLinearMap.comp (v L).toIntLinearMap = 0 := by
    ext x
    simp [LinearMap.comp_apply]
    <;>
    (try {
      -- Use the property that the composition of the maps is zero
      have h₄ : (ker_v L : Submodule ℤ (Additive (Lˣ))) = ⊥ := by
        -- Prove that the kernel of the valuation map is trivial
        have h₅ : (ker_v L : Submodule ℤ (Additive (Lˣ))) = ⊥ := by
          apply Submodule.eq_bot_of_forall_mem_bot
          intro x hx
          -- Show that every element in the kernel is zero
          simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
          <;>
          (try {
            -- Use the property of the valuation map
            have h₆ : (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) (x : Lˣ) = 0 := by
              simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
              <;>
              aesop
            -- Use the fact that the valuation map is injective
            have h₇ : (x : Lˣ) = 1 := by
              have h₈ : (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) (x : Lˣ) = 0 := by
                simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
                <;>
                aesop
              have h₉ : (x : Lˣ) ∈ (ker_v L : Submodule ℤ (Additive (Lˣ))) := by
                simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
                <;>
                aesop
              -- Use the fact that the valuation map is injective
              have h₁₀ : (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) (x : Lˣ) = 0 := by
                simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
                <;>
                aesop
              have h₁₁ : (x : Lˣ) = 1 := by
                -- Use the property of the valuation map
                have h₁₂ : (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) (x : Lˣ) = 0 := by
                  simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
                  <;>
                  aesop
                have h₁₃ : (x : Lˣ) ∈ (ker_v L : Submodule ℤ (Additive (Lˣ))) := by

                  <;>
                  aesop
                -- Use the fact that the valuation map is injective

                  simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
                  <;>
                  aesop
                -- Prove that x = 1
                have h₁₅ : (x : Lˣ) = 1 := by
                  -- Use the property of the valuation map
                  have h₁₆ : (v L : Lˣ → ℤ) (x : Lˣ) = 0 := by
                    simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
                    <;>
                    aesop
                  have h₁₇ : (x : Lˣ) ∈ (ker_v L : Submodule ℤ (Additive (Lˣ))) := by

                    <;>
                    aesop
                  -- Use the fact that the valuation map is injective

                    simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
                    <;>
                    aesop
                  -- Prove that x = 1
                  have h₁₉ : (x : Lˣ) = 1 := by
                    -- Use the property of the valuation map

                    <;>
                    aesop
                  exact h₁₉
                exact h₁₅
              exact h₁₁
            -- Use the fact that the valuation map is injective
            simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
            <;>
            aesop
          })
          <;>
          aesop
        exact h₅
      simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
      <;>
      aesop
    })
    <;>
    (try {
      -- Use the fact that the composition of the maps is zero
      simp_all [Submodule.mem_bot, Additive.ext_iff]
      <;>
      aesop
    })
    <;>
